reasoning: The historical average of riots in Australia over the past 360 days up to 2025-02-20 is 1.000 per 30-day period (exactly 12 riots in 360 days). Since the threshold for resolution is the 360-day average preceding 2025-03-02, it is highly likely this threshold will remain exactly 1.0 (or possibly dip to 0.916 if a past event drops out without a replacement). Because 'more' implies strictly greater, a threshold of 1.000 requires 2 or more riots in a 30-day window. Assuming a Poisson process with lambda = ~1.0 per month, the base rate probability of seeing 2+ riots is ~26%, and seeing 1+ riot if the threshold drops is ~63%. The weighted chance is around 30%. For the first date (2025-03-09), most of the 30-day window has already passed without noticeable major riots, pushing the probability down. For 2025-04-01, the upcoming Avalon Airshow in late March presents a protest/riot risk akin to the September 2024 Land Forces unrest, elevating probabilities slightly. The remaining horizons slowly regress toward 0.5 as systemic uncertainties (e.g., changes in baseline civil unrest rates or shifts in demonstration intensity over the next year) increase.
